item.title: Passionfroot raises $15M to expand its B2B creator marketplace to the US
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Passionfroot, a German startup building a marketplace connecting B2B creators with brands, has raised $15M in a Series A round led by Insight Partners.

item.title: Glow emerges from stealth at $1.2B valuation to challenge endpoint security in the AI era
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Glow is targeting a new class of endpoint risks created by the rapid adoption of AI agents and developer tools inside enterprises.

item.title: Synthesia’s AI training platform is moving beyond videos into live coaching
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Synthesia launched AI Roleplay Sessions, an interactive enterprise training platform where employees practice workplace conversations with AI avatars that provide feedback, scoring, and analytics to help companies measure training effectiveness.

item.title: The Anthropic-Physical Intelligence rumor roiling AI Twitter
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Anthropic and OpenAI's aggressive 2026 acquisition sprees set the stage for a weekend rumor.

item.title: Meta is testing an AI bedtime story app for people with no imagination
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: At last, a tech company has found a way to outsource humanity's oldest pastime: using our imaginations.

item.title: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its pre-release models
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: OpenAI has come forward to claim responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, saying it was the result of internal testing gone awry.

item.title: Jack Dorsey is taking on Slack with Buzz, a group chat platform for teams and their AI agents
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Buzz is a group chat platform for the workplace that puts humans and their AI agents in the same conversation.

item.title: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Over the past decade, streaming platforms competed by dominating individual formats like music, video, podcasts, or audiobooks. Now, as AI makes it easier to create, organize, and recommend content, those distinctions are fading, pushing companies like Spotify, Netflix, YouTube, and TikTok to become all-purpose entertainment destinations instead.

item.title: Data centers expected to use 4x more electricity by 2035
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: New data centers built through 2033 could consume as much electricity as India uses today.

item.title: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

item.title: US threatens sanctions against Chinese AI models over IP theft
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.

item.title: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Deezer said more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily on the platform in June.

item.title: Gritt exits stealth with $32 million for robots to build solar plants — then, everything else
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Gritt is coming out of stealth with $34 million and plans to automate the hardest tasks on construction sites.

item.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

item.title: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.

item.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

item.title: AI’s most important protocol is getting a little bit easier to use
item.dc:creator: Russell Brandom
item.description: Under the new system, the protocol will take a looser, "stateless" approach to session IDs on the server side, similar to how most ordinary websites already work.

item.title: X relaunches a rebuilt Android app after year-long effort
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: X says the rebuilt version of its Android app is now available globally.

item.title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of turning AI into a business.

item.title: Adobe camera app’s new feature will critique your photos using AI
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Adobe's Project Indigo can now remove all kinds of backgrounds from photos you snap using the app.
